yeah agreed. auto compacts seem to have gotten really good lately. but anthropic scams us by defaulting to 1m context which is not only unnecessary due to strong auto-compacts, but also actively degrades performance, just to charge us for more input tokens on every turn

this is similar to early perp exchanges bragging about having up to 100x leverage as if it’s good for anyone other than the exchange

tweets.text:
unfortunately this is nowhere near the worst case scenario

worst case scenario is that the market realizes the btc on their balance sheet isn’t really worth $55 billion, it’s fugazi. the real value is a lot closer to $0 than to $55 billion

the only reason btc is still at $65,000 and not much lower is because saylor keeps buying every week. if he stops buying for a meaningful amount of time i can see btc easily going to $40,000. that’s without him selling a single coin

if he actually tries to sell, in order to raise multi-billion dollars to pay his other obligations, his average sell price will imo be much lower than that. and at that point he will basically have to reinvent the entire company (again) because the jig will be completely up.

tweets.text:
It looks like F3 #Uranium $FUU is valued only based on the JR Zone.

But the Tetra discovery seems to be significant as well.

After reading the latest update, I think the market may be overlooking something even bigger:

The sheer scale of the exploration pipeline across the entire Patterson Lake North project.

$FUU now controls nearly 43,000 hectares in one of the most prospective uranium districts in the world, surrounded by NexGen's Arrow and Paladin's Triple R deposits.

What stood out in this release:

✅ Multiple new drill-ready targets identified across all three PLN properties

✅ Four priority targets around Tetra with geophysical signatures similar to the discovery itself

✅ Historic core sampling returned uranium values and intense alteration, yet several targets remain largely untested

✅ A 7 km-long conductive trend at Minto showing similarities to the conductor hosting the JR Zone

The most important comment in the release came from VP Exploration Sam Hartmann:

Tetra was discovered in an area that many explorers had effectively overlooked.

Instead of following conventional thinking, F3 applied a different geological model and made what became the first uranium discovery in the Clearwater Domain.

Now they're applying that same exploration approach across the broader land package.

That's what makes this exciting.

JR is already a significant discovery.

Tetra continues to grow.

And now the company has multiple additional targets moving into the drill-ready stage.

In the Athabasca Basin, one discovery can create enormous value.

A district with multiple discoveries can create something much larger.

With drilling starting in early July, the next catalyst cycle is just around the corner.

Disclosure: Paid partnership with F3 Uranium. Not financial advice. Please do your own due diligence.

tweets.text:
Many of us are focused on energy security and invest in #silver and #uranium.

But food security and therefore #potash may become just as important.

This latest CEO interview from American Critical Minerals $KCLI makes one thing clear: the United States remains heavily dependent on imported potash, a critical ingredient for global food production.

Its Green River Project in Utah is targeting a potentially massive domestic potash resource in one of North America's most established potash regions.

Highlights:

✅ Exploration target of 500–950 million tonnes of sylvinite

✅ Estimated grades of 19–29% eKCl

✅ Located in Utah with access to infrastructure, roads, rail and power

✅ Potential additional exposure to lithium and bromine

✅ Fully permitted maiden drill program expected to begin this summer

CEO interview 👇

Disclosure: Paid partnership with American Critical Minerals. Not financial advice. Please do your own due diligence.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Dean Pekeski, President & CEO of American Critical Minerals, joins BNN Bloomberg to discuss the company's mining project aiming for #potash, #lithium and #bromine.

Watch here⬇️
https://t.co/X2lI0d3sQA

tweets.text:
If you have a simple idea, take it seriously.

Too often we dismiss our own ideas just because they don’t seem innovative or world-changing enough at first glance.

But that’s not how it works. Almost every massive company today started as something small, unsexy or easy to overlook. Greatness is a process, never the starting point.

So stop caring about what others think and just go build.

Simply starting is already the single biggest step toward success there is.

tweets.text:
When I rip physical packs, I don’t care about most of the cards inside. I’m essentially just chasing a foil Charizard for my collection. So would you call me a collector or a gambler?

Thanks to gachas and claws, we now have the option to sell the cards we don’t want back to the merchant at a discount. The alternative is ending up with a pile of cards I don’t want, left to rot in my mum’s basement.

Redemption rates on platforms like Collector and Beezie beyond 30-40% speak for themselves that collectors actually collect, but grails and not commons.

tweets.text:
The SpaceX IPO is the hottest listing in years.

The best part is you can get access onchain.

Trade it 24/7, long or short, with leverage your broker won't give you.

Here's where to do it:

#1 Synthetic perps

This is for leverage, shorting and 24/7 speculation. You're not owning SpaceX shares. You're trading a contract that tracks the price.

• @HyperliquidX is the main venue right now. $SPCX has become one of the biggest HIP-3 markets and even crossed $2B+ 24H volume.

• • @Aster_DEX has stock perps with high leverage. Currently doing around ~$26.4M 24H volume and ~$3.08M OI on SPCX

• @Lighter_xyz is the ZK perp DEX w/ fast execution and growing equity perp support. ~$35.5M 24H vol with ~$11.1M OI and ~0.039% spread on SPCX

• @StandX_Official lets traders use yield-bearing DUSD as margin while trading perps.

• @extendedapp has been pushing pre-IPO & equity perps with a CEX-like trading experience on @Starknet.

• @PhoenixTrade is the @solana perp w/ low fees, fast execution and fully onchain equity / RWA perps.

• @nadoHQ is building a CLOB-style venue for spot + perps, with the • @inkonchain ecosystem.

#2 Tokenized Spot exposure

If you don't want leverage, here are a few options:

• @OndoFinance has $SPCXon w/ 1:1 SPCX Shares Per Token, available on Eth, BNB Chain, and Solana.

• @Backpack has tokenized $SPCX built around 1:1 backed equity exposure, with Solana-native trading.

• @xStocksFi has $SPCXX, a tracker certificate token that follows SpaceX stock price across Solana and EVM chains.

• @PreStocks is more focused on private / pre-IPO exposure.

Now here's what you're actually trading.

$SPCX is sitting at a massive $2.5T FDV with only ~4.2% of shares actually floating right now.

Massive brand, tiny float, insane attention, and unlocks sitting in the background. Retail's probably going to treat this like a memecoin until more supply opens up.

The bigger story isn't just SpaceX. Every hyped IPO after this probably gets traded the same way.

Some people will use perps for leverage and shorting. Others will use tokenized spot for cleaner exposure.

Both come with their own risks, but the demand is clearly there.

tweets.text:
There's a confirmed airdrop landing between July and September.

The money's already starting to flow in, but there's still time to farm.

It's Kraken's Ink. Over $397M bridged in over the last 30 days. I went through the whole ecosystem and here's some opportunities I see:

Kraken

Start with @krakenpro. It's the most direct play since you earn Ink points for trading volume, deposits, and staking.

Ink is Kraken's own L2, so my bet is Kraken users get the heaviest weighting when the criteria drop. That's why it's first on my list.

Tydro

@tydrohq is the lazy farm. It's the largest native lending protocol on Ink, and the mechanic's dead simple: more lending and borrowing means more points.

If you want to keep it passive, this is the one to park activity in.

Nado

@nadoHQ is the one that's actually got me interested. It's been generating the most daily revenue on Ink recently, which is probably what's driving the inflow surge in the first place.

But here's what makes it different. On Nado you earn Nado points, not INK points, and the team confirmed those convert to $INK at TGE.

So there's a real chance you're farming two potential airdrops off one position, Ink and Nado.

Season 2 is running now with points going out weekly, and they launched a $100k trading comp yesterday.

xStocks

One more protocol I'd layer in: @xStocksFi doesn't have a direct INK farming mechanism, but it integrates with Nado, so you can use tokenized equities as collateral on Nado and stack xStocks points on top of everything else.

2 birds one stone without much effort.

Timing

@buyerofponzi advises both Ink and Nado, and he confirmed the July to September window in an interview with @nothreadguy.

The best airdrops close before most people hear about them, and this is shaping up to be one of those.

Get positioned now.

tweets.text:
Glad you liked it! I’d say it’s easy enough for a terminally online crypto user to buy their own basket, but many people don’t have the time or desire to do that and would gladly outsource it to someone else — I don’t get why it has to be an anon tho, why can’t trusted/known personalities (or even companies) launch their own indexes?

There are still issues with the design and i also don’t see it taking off as it stands, but with one tweaks/improvements I think there’s a lot of merit to the concept

Maybe it’s prism/spectrum that figures it out, maybe it’s some other project in the future, but onchain index funds still seem like a great great great concept to me

tweets.text: A dot is just one piece of data from one moment in time; keep that in perspective as you synthesize. Just as you need to sort big from small, and what's happening in the moment from overall patterns, you need to know how much learning you can get out of any one dot without overweighing it. #principleoftheday

tweets.text:
Liya Ngabola, a student at Ensign College, is raising chickens and creating jobs in Zimbabwe.

And she just won $20,000 at the 2026 Tim Draper Utah Entrepreneur Challenge!

Her company, Shurugwi Prime Breeders, raises chickens and creates jobs in Zimbabwe.

The challenge awarded $75,000+ to student founders across the state, and 20 teams walked away with money, mentorship, and momentum.

Young people aren’t just the future of entrepreneurship, they’re the present too!

Congratulations to every team that competed and to the Lassonde Entrepreneur Institute at the University of Utah for running a great student entrepreneur program.

Thanks to the Salt Lake Business Journal for covering the event!

https://t.co/GV0k6PxYla

tweets.text:
It's beyond stupid to expect STRC to die while encouraging people to buy BTC

That's probably my main point about the MSTR criticism: if you want to be intellectually consistent, tell people that BTC is outright doomed until Saylor is greatly damaged, but thinking that somehow buying BTC is a good idea while MSTR will die in the years to come is just dumb

tweets.text:
The discrepancy is getting even larger, with STRC ticking sub $92 yesterday while SATA is trading at par.

SATA is 'only' paying 1.5% more per year, which shouldn't be enough to justify such a difference, I'm personally convinced that the market has no clue how to price these prefs correctly.

The nature of the holder base as well as the market cap of the pref does matter a lot. STRC increased from like $3bn mcap to $10bn mcap in 3 months, which implies that there is a LOT of rotations (from old/panicking to new holders) needed during each "depeg" to get back to par.

Strategy will increase their dividend rate to 12% in July, then probably even more in August. STRC will end up trading back to par, but it can take time. If BTC dumps further down, it will take EVEN more time.

But as long as STRC is trading below par, there are no inflows into BTC from STRC, so at least we can see BTC trade 'organically' without people trying to frontrun the STRC inflows.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
SATA has started the daily dividends and is now trading at par.

STRC is still at $95 because its market cap is so much larger (needs a lot more of hands changing), but if BTC has bottomed, it will get back to par too.

BTC not trading based on STRC flows is great. https://t.co/LOfnfKD5tc

SATA is 'only' paying 1.5% more per year, which shouldn't be enough to justify such a difference, I'm personally convinced that the market has no clue how to price these prefs correctly.

The nature of the holder base as well as the market cap of the pref does matter a lot. STRC increased from like $3bn mcap to $10bn mcap in 3 months, which implies that there is a LOT of rotations (from old/panicking to new holders) needed during each "depeg" to get back to par.

Strategy will increase their dividend rate to 12% in July, then probably even more in August. STRC will end up trading back to par, but it can take time. If BTC dumps further down, it will take EVEN more time.

But as long as STRC is trading below par, there are no inflows into BTC from STRC, so at least we can see BTC trade 'organically' without people trying to frontrun the STRC inflows.

tweets.text:
Something I’m wondering as I’m beginning to learn piano is how likely is it in the next 30 years that we could inject « skills » like « top piano players » via brain-computer interfaces similar to Neuralink? 

Like an artificial neuron (AI) enhancing my not artificial neurons? 

Some options:

- The device could detect I’m about to hit the wrong note and correct my motor intent before I even make the mistake.

- Not injecting skill but compressing the time it takes to learn. I could practice 10 minutes instead of 10 years. 

- The device modifies my synapses connections for me to become a top piano player right away 

There is a sort of nihilism associated with AGI where I’m always thinking: is that worth it or the model will help me do it in minutes instead of years soon? 🫠
